steps.title: Compute f(1)
steps.content: All four forms describe the same function, so they share the same $f(1) = k$. Use (B): $f(1) = 42(1.2)^1 = 50.4$. So $k = 50.4$.

steps.title: Find the form where k appears
steps.content:
We need a form whose coefficient or base equals $50.4$. Check (C):

$$f(x) = 50.4(1.2)^{x-1}$$

Coefficient is $50.4 = k$. (Verify $f(1) = 50.4(1.2)^0 = 50.4$ ✓.)

That matches choice C — C is correct.
